_source: Derived from Scala CoreDataSerializer wire format spec (putUShort VLQ length || signed two's-complement payload). See README.md in this directory for the full derivation table and oracle provenance.
_format: Each entry: { value: human-readable description, signed_bytes_hex: hex of BigInteger.toByteArray signed payload, wire_hex: full wire bytes (VLQ length prefix || payload), should_round_trip: bool }

vectors.note: BigInteger.ZERO.toByteArray() = [0x00] (one byte); wire = VLQ len(1) || 0x00 = [0x01, 0x00]. The reader also accepts an alternate len=0 encoding defensively, but Scala emits the 1-byte form for SBigInt
Largest negative value (-2^255). Fits in 32 signed bytes; sign bit is set in the top byte
Largest positive value (+2^255 - 1). Top byte 0x7F keeps the sign bit clear

write_rejections.reason: 33-byte signed encoding (leading 0x00 to keep sign bit clear); exceeds MaxBigIntSizeInBytes = 32

read_rejections.description: len=65535 VLQ-encoded; the cap MUST fire before payload is read
